sitar.name: Sitar
sitar.bleed_pattern: Sustained harmonics that overlap with vocal formants. Sympathetic strings create high-frequency shimmer.

tabla.name: Tabla
tabla.bleed_pattern: Sharp attack transients (2-5 kHz) can bleed into vocal onset. Bass resonance (60-200 Hz) can color vocal low-end.

harmonium.name: Harmonium
harmonium.bleed_pattern: CRITICAL: Reed frequencies (200-800 Hz) directly overlap with male vocal fundamental range. Hardest instrument to separate from baritone voices like Mehdi Hassan.

tanpura.name: Tanpura (Tambura)
tanpura.bleed_pattern: Continuous drone provides sustained harmonic bed. Jivari (buzzing bridge) creates broadband high-frequency content that is spectrally distinct from vocals.

dhol.name: Dhol
dhol.bleed_pattern: Extremely broadband instrument (50 Hz - 8 kHz). Treble side attacks are spectrally similar to vocal sibilants. Bass side can mask low vocal energy.

sarangi.name: Sarangi
sarangi.bleed_pattern: CRITICAL: Called 'the instrument closest to the human voice'. Fundamental range almost perfectly overlaps female vocal range. The hardest instrument to separate from female vocals (Noor Jehan).

electric_guitar.name: Electric Guitar
electric_guitar.bleed_pattern: Distorted guitar occupies broad spectrum. Demucs is well-trained on Western rock guitars.

synthesizer.name: Synthesizer / Keyboard
synthesizer.bleed_pattern: Synth pads can occupy same frequency range as vocals. Common in Nazia Hassan era disco recordings.

male_baritone.note: Mehdi Hassan, Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan lower register

female_soprano.note: Nazia Hassan, higher register film singers
